Global (All).summary: China expresses concern over Taiwan\u2019s participation in US-led summit
Russia\u2019s Gazprom confirms cuts in gas deliveries to Europe
Taliban ban on girls\u2019 education sparks international criticism, protests

United States.title: US Federal Reserve Hikes Interest Rates Amid Inflation Concerns
United States.summary: The US Federal Reserve raised interest rates by 0.75% in an effort to combat inflation, sparking concerns about the impact on the economy.

United States.title: Gun Violence Escalates in US with Multiple Shootings
United States.summary: A series of shootings across the US has left several dead and injured, highlighting the ongoing issue of gun violence and its impact on society.

United States.title: US Supreme Court Ruling on Abortion Sparks Protests
United States.summary: The US Supreme Court's decision to overturn Roe v. Wade has sparked widespread protests and concerns about the erosion of reproductive rights.

South Korea.title: North Korea Fires Cruise Missiles into Sea Off Eastern Coast
South Korea.summary: North Korea test-fired cruise missiles into the sea off its eastern coast, a move seen as a challenge to South Korea's new government and a potential provocation to the US.

South Korea.title: South Korean Unions Call for General Strike Ahead of Government Economic Reforms
South Korea.summary: South Korean labor unions have called for a general strike against the government's economic reforms, which they claim will exacerbate income inequality and hurt workers' livelihoods.

South Korea.title: South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol Warns North Korea Could Cause a 'Catastrophic War'
South Korea.summary: South Korea's President Yoon Suk-yeol has warned that North Korea's nuclear threats could lead to a 'catastrophic war' that could spread globally.

Japan.title: Protests against planned nuclear facilities in Japan
Japan.summary: Residents in the city of Omaezaki were protesting against the planned construction of new nuclear facilities, citing concerns about safety and environmental impact. The protests have been ongoing for several days and have received significant media attention. Japan's nuclear industry has faced intense scrutiny since the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster, and any new developments are likely to be met with resistance.

Japan.title: Japan's inflation rate reaches 8-year high
Japan.summary: Japan's inflation rate has surged to an 8-year high, with prices rising 3.8% year-over-year in May. The inflation rate has been driven by increasing costs for food, housing, and transportation. This has led to concerns about the impact on consumers and the broader economy, with some economists warning of a potential 'death spiral' of rising prices and stagnant wages.

Japan.title: Former Japanese diplomat arrested on terrorism charges
Japan.summary: A former Japanese diplomat was arrested in Tokyo on charges of providing funds to a terrorist organization. The diplomat, who was stationed in the Middle East, is accused of transferring money to a group linked to extremism. The arrest has sparked concerns about the potential for terrorism financing and the need for increased security measures.
